skill.name: doc-sync
skill.description: Keeps IdeaVim documentation aligned with code changes by checking examples, signatures, file paths, and recent git history against working implementations.
skill.summary: Verifies and updates IdeaVim documentation against current code behavior.
skill.author: JetBrains

security_audit.summary: The confirmed risk is legitimate but elevated command guidance: the skill directs the assistant to run grep, find, and git commands while auditing documentation. The static weak-cryptography and network-scanning alerts are false positives from words such as description and Vim mapping syntax, with no evidence of malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Repository Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs assistants to run grep, find, git log, and git show commands against the repository. This is appropriate for documentation verification, but it increases operational risk because commands inspect the local filesystem and git history.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and serve the stated documentation-sync workflow. I found no evidence that they exfiltrate data or execute untrusted generated shell strings.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Several external-command alerts come from Markdown inline code or fenced examples, not Ruby-style backtick execution. These examples describe files and workflow steps rather than executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown and the backticks delimit paths, examples, and report text. No executable Ruby code or shell backtick expansion exists in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts match ordinary documentation words and headings, not cryptographic functions. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive prose and an output template. They do not include cryptographic APIs, hashes, encryption calls, or security-sensitive transformations.

low_findings.title: Network Scanning Alert Is Vim Mapping Syntax
low_findings.description: The network-scanning alert is caused by the string nmap inside a grep pattern for Vim mapping documentation. In this context, nmap means a Vim normal-mode mapping command, not a network scanner.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a grep pattern that searches documentation for Vim mapping commands such as nmap, vmap, and map. It does not invoke the nmap network scanning tool.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell and Git Commands in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes bash examples and inline instructions for repository inspection. These commands should be reviewed before execution, especially when placeholders are replaced with user-provided paths or commit identifiers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are plainly present and are intended to be run during the workflow. They are common read-only repository commands, so the danger is operational rather than malicious.

content.value_statement: Code changes can leave IdeaVim documentation examples inaccurate. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused checks against real implementations.
actual_capabilities: Compares documentation examples with current IdeaVim implementation patterns.
Searches documentation for function signatures, mapping commands, and named parameters.
Uses recent git history to identify removals, deprecations, and breaking changes.
Supports documentation-to-code verification and code-change-to-documentation updates.
Produces a documentation sync report with checked files, discrepancies, updates, and notes.
limitations: It is tailored to the IdeaVim repository structure and documentation locations.
It depends on readable source files and available git history.
It may need human review for ambiguous API behavior or intentional documentation differences.
It does not replace automated tests for code examples.

use_cases.title: Verify Changed Documentation
use_cases.description: Check whether a specific IdeaVim documentation file still matches current implementation details.

use_cases.title: Review API Change Impact
use_cases.description: Inspect recent code changes and identify documentation examples that need updates.

use_cases.title: Prepare Release Documentation
use_cases.description: Audit key documentation before release to catch removed parameters, renamed files, or stale examples.

prompt_templates.title: Check One Doc File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for a known documentation file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether doc/ideavim-mappings.md matches the current IdeaVim code. Report discrepancies and update only clearly incorrect examples.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Code Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review after editing a source file.
prompt_templates.prompt: I changed MappingScope.kt. Find related IdeaVim documentation, compare examples with working implementations, and update stale references.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Recent Removals
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced check focused on breaking changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review recent commits for removed or deprecated IdeaVim APIs. Then check documentation for affected examples and summarize required updates.

prompt_templates.title: Run Comprehensive Sync
prompt_templates.prompt: Check all files in the doc folder against current IdeaVim implementation patterns. Prioritize code examples, signatures, named parameters, and file paths.

output_examples.input: Check if doc/ideavim-mappings.md is in sync with the code.
output: Files checked: mapping documentation and current mapping implementation files.
Discrepancy found: one documented parameter no longer exists in the current API.
Update made: the example now matches the working implementation pattern.

output_examples.input: I changed MappingScope.kt, check if docs need updating.
output: Files checked: changed source file, recent git history, and related documentation.
No documentation update required: public usage remained unchanged.
Note: internal implementation changed, but documented examples still match.

best_practices: Start from working code before editing documentation.
Verify every named parameter and function signature in examples.
Keep documentation edits narrow and consistent with existing style.
anti_patterns: Updating wording when the documented behavior is already accurate.
Trusting documentation examples without comparing them to current code.
Making broad documentation rewrites for a small API change.

faq.question: What repositories is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for the IdeaVim repository and its doc, README, and contributing files.

faq.question: Can it update documentation automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. It can update documentation when it finds clear mismatches with current implementation behavior.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: It may guide Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to run read-only grep, find, and git commands.

faq.question: Can it verify code examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks examples, signatures, parameters, and usage patterns against working implementations.

faq.question: Does it handle non-IdeaVim projects?
faq.answer: Not directly. Its workflow and paths are specific to IdeaVim documentation and Kotlin implementation patterns.

faq.question: When should a human review the result?
faq.answer: A human should review ambiguous behavior changes, public API decisions, and any broad documentation edits.
